commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends agent session write-lock calls to publish owned transcript writes for message persistence and auto-compaction, with focused session-lock tests.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +55. Total +68 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the PR head shows AgentSession sends `{ publishOwnedWrite: true }`, but the embedded runner’s production callback still accepts only `operation` and discards the second argument before it reaches the controller.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: forward the new write-lock options through the embedded runner and add coverage for that exact production path.

target.title: fix(agents): publish auto-compaction transcript writes
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). This is in progress; the fix has local regression proof and is deployed to the private HF test Space, with one post-deploy inbound Telegram confirmation still pending.

AI-assisted change.

## Summary

Telegram users could hit a generic error after a normal run crossed the auto-compaction threshold.
OpenClaw appended its own compaction row while the embedded prompt lock was released, then the session fence treated that valid internal write as an external takeover.
This change lets auto-compaction publish that write as OpenClaw-owned, preserving takeover protection while allowing the session file to advance.

## What Changed

The embedded session lock already has a path for accepting owned transcript writes during prompt execution.
Auto-compaction was using the same session file but did not publish its append through that path.

- Extended `AgentSessionWriteLockRunner` so callers can request owned-write publication.
- Forwarded the write-lock options through `AgentSession.runWithSessionWriteLock`.
- Wrapped the compaction-row append in `runCompactionWork()` with `{ publishOwnedWrite: true }`.
- Added focused session-lock coverage for compaction appends published through the owned transcript write context.

## Testing

The focused regression test and the core test typecheck pass on this branch after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.
A fixed image from the same code path was also deployed to the private Hugging Face Space and reached a healthy startup state.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Real behavior proof:

Behavior addressed: Telegram message processing failed after auto-compaction appended a compaction row during an embedded prompt run.

Real environment tested: Private Hugging Face Space `osolmaz/onurclawtest` on `cpu-upgrade`, private bucket volume `osolmaz/onurclawtest-data`, Telegram polling channel enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and pushed `ghcr.io/osolmaz/openclaw-live-test:hf-53e662fb`, updated the Space Dockerfile, pushed Space commit `0251267002af2274cdd289185f2beaf053ae084b`, then checked Space status and logs with `hf spaces info osolmaz/onurclawtest` and `hf spaces logs osolmaz/onurclawtest --tail 300`.

Evidence after fix: Space reached `RUNNING`; startup logs showed Telegram DNS/TCP/TLS success, `fetch getMe ok`, gateway ready, model `huggingface/Qwen/Qwen3-8B`, and Telegram polling started.

Observed result after fix: The deployment starts cleanly with the fixed image and the Telegram provider connects. The prior failure signature was tied to an OpenClaw-owned compaction row being treated as takeover; the regression test now exercises that exact fence behavior.

What was not tested: A new inbound Telegram message after the Space rebuild has not yet been sent/confirmed in this PR body. This PR is draft until that final live message proof is added.

## Risks

The change is narrow, but it touches session locking behavior.
The main risk is accepting too much as owned, so the test keeps the protection boundary explicit.

- The new accepted path requires the existing owned transcript write context and `publishOwnedWrite` option.
- Existing tests still cover rejecting unowned external compaction edits before prompt reacquire.
- External session takeover detection remains active.

## Follow-ups

Add the final post-deploy Telegram message proof before marking ready for review.
target.author: osolmaz

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
